title: Shish taouk
subtitle: Lebanese yoghurt-marinated chicken skewers
excerpt: Boneless chicken thighs marinated in yoghurt, garlic, tomato paste, and lemon, then threaded on skewers and grilled or griddled until the outside is charred and the inside is juicy. A Lebanese street-food standard served with garlic sauce, pitta, and pickles.
sourceNotes: Shish taouk is a Lebanese and broadly Levantine marinated chicken kebab, with the marinade typically based on yoghurt, tomato paste, and garlic. It is documented as a staple of Lebanese street food and restaurant culture in Anissa Helou's 'Lebanese Cuisine' (1994). The garlic sauce served alongside (toum) is the defining condiment.

content.text: The tomato paste in the marinade is what separates shish taouk from other yoghurt-marinated chicken dishes. It adds a concentrated savoury sweetness that caramelises on the grill and produces a darker, more complex crust than a simple yoghurt marinade. Combined with the garlic and lemon, the marinade seasons the meat thoroughly over 4 hours. Use boneless thighs, not breasts. They stay moist on a hot griddle where breasts would dry out.

The griddle pan or grill needs to be very hot before the chicken goes on. Each side needs 6 to 7 minutes of contact without moving. If you lift the chicken before it has formed a crust, it will tear. When it releases cleanly from the grill, it is ready to turn.

items.name: Chicken thigh
items.prepNote: boneless, skinless, cut into 4 cm pieces

content.text: Mix the {{plain-yoghurt}}, {{tomato-puree}}, {{garlic}}, {{lemon}} juice, {{cumin-ground}}, {{allspice}}, {{paprika-sweet}}, {{extra-virgin-olive-oil}}, and salt together. Add the {{chicken-thigh}} pieces and coat well. Cover and refrigerate for at least 4 hours. Thread the chicken pieces onto metal or soaked wooden skewers, packing them closely.

Heat a griddle pan or outdoor grill to the highest heat. Cook the skewers for 6 to 7 minutes per side, without moving them, until the chicken has grill marks and the marinade has charred at the edges. The chicken is cooked when the juices run clear when pierced at the thickest point. Rest for 5 minutes. Serve with warm pitta, garlic sauce, and pickles.

items.fix: Wait until the chicken releases cleanly when you attempt to lift it, this takes 6 to 7 minutes. Force-lifting tears the crust and causes sticking
items.cause: Pan not hot enough or chicken moved too soon

items.fix: Thighs only for this dish. Check for doneness at 12 minutes total, the juices should run clear but the meat should still feel slightly springy rather than firm
Shake off excess marinade before threading, a thick coating steams the chicken rather than allowing it to char. The grill must be smoking hot
items.cause: Pan not at full heat or marinade applied too thickly

items.fix: Taste the marinade before adding the chicken. It should be assertively seasoned, the flavour dilutes as it soaks into the meat
items.cause: Too little salt or garlic, or marination time too short

content.text: Shish taouk is the chicken counterpart to the lamb shish kebab and has an equally deep place in Lebanese and broader Levantine street-food culture. The word 'taouk' is Turkish for 'chicken', which reflects the Ottoman origin of the vertical spit tradition from which both shish taouk and shawarma descend. On the streets of Beirut, shish taouk is almost always served with toum, the Lebanese garlic sauce that is made by slowly emulsifying raw garlic with oil and lemon juice into something between a mayonnaise and a spread. Without the toum, the dish is good; with it, it is something else.
